title: A Pindaric Ode by Ben Jonson — Love Poetry Lines
content:
THE TURN
Brave infant of Saguntum, clear
Thy coming forth in that great year,
When the prodigious Hannibal did crown
His rage with razing your immortal town.
Thou looking then about,
Ere thou wert half got out,
Wise child, didst hastily return,
meaning: "A Pindaric Ode" by Ben Jonson is a love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 164 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "THE TURN Brave infant of Saguntum, clear...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ben Jonson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
